title: Find the mistake in one-step equations
description: Practice spotting the mistake in someone else's work as they attempt to solve one-step equations.
topic_path: Find the mistake in one-step equations

assessment_items.question_content:
Carly tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
7a&=28\\\\
\dfrac{7a}a&=\dfrac{28}7&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
7&=4&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Carly make their first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Carly needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Carly needs to correctly solve the equation they $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Carly both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then they correctly solved the equation.

When $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$ the equation for solving, Carly divided the left side of the equation by $a$ and the right side of the equation by $7$.

This is a mistake!

Carly should have divided both sides of the equation by $7$.

###Answer

Carly's first mistake was in $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Alanna tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
4c&=12\\\\
\dfrac{4c}{4}&=12\cdot4&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
c&=48&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Alanna make her first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Alanna needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Alanna needs to correctly solve the equation she $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Alanna both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then she correctly solved the equation.

When $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$ the equation for solving, Alanna divided the left side of the equation by $4$ and multiplied the right side of the equation by $4$.

This is a mistake!

Alanna should have divided both sides of the equation by $4$.

###Answer

Alanna's first mistake was in $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Trent tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac g{3}&=\dfrac43\\\\
\dfrac{g}{3} \cdot 3&=\dfrac43\cdot\dfrac13&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
g&=\dfrac49&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Trent make his first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Trent needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Trent needs to correctly solve the equation he $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Trent both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then he correctly solved the equation.

When $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$ the equation for solving, Trent multiplied the left side of the equation by $3$ and the right side of the equation by $\dfrac{1}{3}$.

This is a mistake!

Trent should have multiplied both sides of the equation by $3$.

###Answer

Trent's first mistake was in $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Subira tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac h{0.5}&=7\\\\
\dfrac{h}{0.5} \cdot 0.5&=\dfrac7{0.5}&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
h&=14&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Subira make her first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Subira needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Subira needs to correctly solve the equation she $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Subira both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then she correctly solved the equation.

When $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$ the equation for solving, Subira multiplied the left side of the equation by $0.5$ and divided the right side of the equation by $0.5$.

This is a mistake!

Subira should have multiplied both sides of the equation by $0.5$.

###Answer

Subira's first mistake was in $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Oscar tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac34+m&=\dfrac54\\\\
\dfrac34+m-\dfrac34&=\dfrac54+\dfrac34&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
m&=2&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Oscar make his first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Oscar needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Oscar needs to correctly solve the equation he $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Oscar both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then he correctly solved the equation.

When $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$ the equation for solving, Oscar subtracted $\dfrac{3}{4}$ from the left side of the equation and added $\dfrac{3}{4}$ to the right side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

Oscar should have subtracted $\dfrac{3}{4}$ from both sides of the equation.

###Answer

Oscar's first mistake was in $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ling tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
12&=p+6.2\\\\
12+6.2&=p+6.2-6.2&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
18.2&=p&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Ling make their first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Ling needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Ling needs to correctly solve the equation they $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Ling both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then they correctly solved the equation.

When $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$ the equation for solving, Ling added $6.2$ to the left side of the equation and subtracted $6.2$ from the right side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

Ling should have subtracted $6.2$ from both sides of the equation.

###Answer

Ling's first mistake was in $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Katy tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
r-3&=14.3\\\\
r-3+3&=14.3-3&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
r&=11.3&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Katy make her first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Katy needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Katy needs to correctly solve the equation she $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Katy both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then she correctly solved the equation.

When $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$ the equation for solving, Katy added $3$ to the left side of the equation and subtracted $3$ from the right side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

Katy should have added $3$ to both sides of the equation.

###Answer

Katy's first mistake was in $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nasir tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
7.7&=w-2\\\\
7.7-2&=w-2+2&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
5.7&=w&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Nasir make his first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Nasir needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Nasir needs to correctly solve the equation he $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Nasir both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then he correctly solved the equation.

When $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$ the equation for solving, Nasir subtracted $2$ from the left side of the equation and added $2$ to the right side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

Nasir should have added $2$ to both sides of the equation.

###Answer

Nasir's first mistake was in $\tealE{\text{setting up}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Marcus tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
2.5b&=5\\\\
\dfrac{2.5b}{2.5}&=\dfrac{5}{2.5}&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
b&=3&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Marcus make his first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Marcus needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Marcus needs to correctly solve the equation he $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Marcus both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then he correctly solved the equation.

Marcus divided both sides of the equation by $2.5$ to get $b$ by itself.

He correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Marcus then evaluated $\dfrac{5}{2.5}$ to get $3$.

This is a mistake!

$\dfrac5{2.5}$ equals $2$, not $3$.

He did not correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Marcus's first mistake was in $\purpleD{\text{calculating}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Derek tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
8d&=72\\\\
\dfrac{8d}{8}&=\dfrac{72}8&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
d&=8&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Derek make his first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Derek needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Derek needs to correctly solve the equation he $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Derek both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then he correctly solved the equation.

Derek divided both sides of the equation by $8$ to get $d$ by itself.

He correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Derek then evaluated $\dfrac{72}{8}$ to get $8$.

This is a mistake!

$\dfrac{72}{8}$ equals $9$, not $8$ .

He did not correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Derek's first mistake was in $\purpleD{\text{calculating}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Gary tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac e{4}&=2.5\\\\
\dfrac{e}4 \cdot4&=2.5\cdot4&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
e&=8&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Gary make his first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Gary needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Gary needs to correctly solve the equation he $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Gary both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then he correctly solved the equation.

Gary multiplied both sides of the equation by $4$ to get $e$ by itself.

He correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Gary then evaluated $2.5\cdot4$ to get $8$.

This is a mistake!

$2.5\cdot 4 = 10$, not $8$.

He did not correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Gary's first mistake was in $\purpleD{\text{calculating}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rita tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
n+12&=18.3\\\\
n+12-12&=18.3-12&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
n&=5.7&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Rita make her first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Rita needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Rita needs to correctly solve the equation she $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Rita both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then she correctly solved the equation.

Rita subtracted $12$ from both sides of the equation to get $n$ by itself.

She correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Rita then evaluated $18.3-12$ to get $5.7$.

This is a mistake!

$18.3-12$ equals $6.3$, not $5.7$.

She did not correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Rita's first mistake was in $\purpleD{\text{calculating}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Quinn tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac43&=q+\dfrac13\\\\
\dfrac43-\dfrac13&=q+\dfrac13-\dfrac13&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
\dfrac23&=q&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Quinn make their first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Quinn needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Quinn needs to correctly solve the equation they $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Quinn both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then they correctly solved the equation.

Quinn subtracted $\dfrac{1}{3}$ from both sides of the equation to get $q$ by itself.

They correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Quinn then evaluated $\dfrac43-\dfrac13$ to get  $\dfrac23$.

This is a mistake!

$\dfrac43-\dfrac13$ equals $\dfrac33$ or $1$, not $\dfrac23$.

They did not correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Quinn's first mistake was in $\purpleD{\text{calculating}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Emma tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
t-\dfrac35&=\dfrac45\\\\
t-\dfrac35+\dfrac35&=\dfrac45+\dfrac35&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
t&=1&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Emma make her first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Emma needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Emma needs to correctly solve the equation she $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Emma both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then she correctly solved the equation.

Emma added $\dfrac{3}{5}$ to both sides of the equation to get $t$ by itself.

She correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Emma then evaluated $\dfrac45+\dfrac35$ to get  $1$.

This is a mistake!

$\dfrac45+\dfrac35$ equals $\dfrac75$, not $1$.

She did not correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Emma's first mistake was in $\purpleD{\text{calculating}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ofra tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
3x&=4.5\\\\
\dfrac{3x}3&=\dfrac{4.5}3&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
x&=1.5&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Ofra make her first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Ofra needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

If Ofra both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then she correctly solved the equation.

Ofra divided both sides of the equation by $3$ to get $x$ by itself.

She correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Ofra then evaluated $\dfrac{4.5}{3}$ to get  $1.5$.

She correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Ofra correctly solved the equation.

Paulo multiplied both sides of the equation by $0.25$ to get $f$ by itself.

Paulo then evaluated $16\cdot0.25$ to get  $4$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jackie tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
7.5+k&=10\\\\
7.5+k-7.5&=10-7.5&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
k&=2.5&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Jackie make their first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Jackie needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Jackie needs to correctly solve the equation they $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Jackie both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then they correctly solved the equation.

Jackie subtracted $7.5$ from both sides of the equation  to get $k$ by itself.

They correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Jackie then evaluated $10-7.5$ to get  $2.5$.

They correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Jackie correctly solved the equation.

assessment_items.question_content:
Veronica tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
8&=y-4.6\\\\
8+4.6&=y-4.6+4.6&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
12.6&=y&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Veronica make her first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Veronica needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Veronica needs to correctly solve the equation she $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Veronica both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then she correctly solved the equation.

Veronica added $4.6$  to both sides of the equation to get $y$ by itself.

She correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Veronica then evaluated $8+4.6$ to get  $12.6$.

She correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution.

###Answer

Veronica correctly solved the equation.

assessment_items.question_content:
Zach tried to solve an equation.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac34&=z-\dfrac12\\\\
\dfrac34+\dfrac12&=z-\dfrac12+\dfrac12&\tealE{\text{Setting up}}\\\\
\dfrac54&=z&\purpleD{\text{Calculating}}
\end{align}$

**Where did Zach make his first mistake?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Strategy

To $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation for solving, Zach needs to perform the same operation on both sides of the equation.

To $\purpleD{\text{calculate}}$ the solution, Zach needs to correctly solve the equation he $\tealE{\text{set up}}$.

If Zach both $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution correctly, then he correctly solved the equation.

Zach added $\dfrac{1}{2}$ to both sides of the equation to get $z$ by itself.

He correctly $\tealE{\text{set up}}$ the equation.

Zach then evaluated $\dfrac34+\dfrac12$ to get  $\dfrac54$.

He correctly $\purpleD{\text{calculated}}$ the solution.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Answer

Zach correctly solved the equation.
